## 2. Session Pattern Issues

### Recurring blocker: Patrick git push accumulation
Sessions 140, 142, 143, 144, 146 all contain explicit blocker notes that staged files are waiting for Patrick to run `.\push.ps1`. Session 146 specifically flags the session 145 commit block as still pending. This pattern has repeated across at least 5 consecutive sessions without a resolution mechanism — there is no handoff that forces push resolution before the next session begins.

### Recurring blocker: Skill install deferred
Skills packaged in sessions 140, 142, 143, 144, 146 all required Patrick to install them via Cowork UI. Sessions 142 and 143 each shipped 7+ new skills. No audit mechanism confirms installed vs. pending — skill install status is tracked informally in session-log notes only, not in a durable manifest.

### Camera v2 dev handoff not consumed (>24h)
MESSAGE_BOARD message `msg-146-architect-decisions-final` (timestamp 2026-03-11T18:50:00Z) is in state `feedbackLoop: "ready-for-dev-dispatch"`. Sessions 147 and 148 were both diverted to rapidfire bug fixes and did not pick up this handoff. The architecture is complete, approved, and waiting. Age: ~17 hours as of audit.

## 5. Token Budget Accuracy Trend

**Insufficient data due to manifest staleness.**

Only 3 sessions have complete records:
- Session 118: ~110k (est) — high burn, 7 subagents, no manifest
- Session 142: ~35k (est) — Phase 1 fleet redesign
- Session 143: ~55k (est) — Phase 2 fleet redesign, `budgetDelta: "succeeded-on-plan"`

Sessions 144–148 have no token records. The manifest `currentSession` block was never cleared/updated at session start for sessions 144–148 — it shows session 147 stub data that was never written over by an actual session start checkpoint.

**Trend verdict:** Cannot assess. Manifest maintenance has lapsed. The checkpoint discipline that was established in session 116–119 has not been maintained through the fleet redesign sessions.

---

## 6. Recommendations

**Recommendation 1 — Catch up the manifest (next session start)**
At the top of the next session, update `.checkpoint-manifest.json` to log sessions 144–148 in sessionHistory with estimated token burns and file counts from session-log.md. Then establish a hard rule: checkpoint manifest write is the first and last action of every session, not optional.

**Recommendation 2 — Process the camera v2 dev handoff**
MESSAGE_BOARD `msg-146-architect-decisions-final` has been waiting >24h. The next dev session should either dispatch findasale-dev on the camera workflow v2 implementation or explicitly defer with a dated note in STATE.md so the message board doesn't accumulate stale "ready" items.

**Recommendation 3 — Create a session-start git push confirmation gate**
The recurring "Patrick needs to push" blocker across 5+ sessions indicates there's no forcing function. Add a `conversation-defaults` rule (or next-session-prompt standard block) that asks Patrick at session start: "Are there staged files from last session waiting for push?" and lists them if so. This converts an implicit blocker into an explicit gate.

---

## Patrick Direct

**⚠️ P0 — Vercel auto-deploy is disconnected**

As of sessions 147–148 (2026-03-12), the Vercel GitHub App integration is broken. Frontend pushes to GitHub main are **not** triggering Vercel deployments. This means:
- The nested `<button>` fix (commit `622195faa`) is on GitHub but **not live** for users
- Any future frontend code that ships will silently not deploy

**Action required (Patrick only):** Go to Vercel dashboard → findasale project → Settings → Git → reconnect the GitHub App integration. Or trigger a manual deployment from the Vercel dashboard using "Redeploy" on the latest commit. This blocks all frontend fixes from reaching production until resolved.

**Supporting evidence:** STATE.md Known Gotchas: *"Vercel GitHub App integration — As of session 148 (2026-03-12), Vercel is not auto-deploying on push."*
